{{Quote|Viceroy, we have acquired target: Naboo settlement, New Centrif.|OOM-9|audio=NewCentrifSeen.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
'''New Centrif''' was a [[City/Legends|city]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] inhabited by [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. It contained a [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitter]] for off-world communications as well as residential and military buildings and processing centers for natural resources such as [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]], [[Nova crystal/Legends|nova crystals]], and [[Ore/Legends|ore]]. The city was considered well defended with [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], [[Tuskcat Trooper]]s, and [[V-19 landspeeder]]s protecting it along with two [[Turret/Legends|turrets]] at its gated northern entrance.

In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] targeted the New Centrif transmitter in the first stage of their [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], and [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] sent the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droid]] [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] with a small task force to carry the [[Subjugation of Parrlay|attack]] out. OOM-9 chose not to attack New Centrif first despite landing close to it, instead directing his forces toward the nearby city of [[Vis/Legends|Vis]] to destroy its transmitter. There, OOM-9 gained reinforcements from the [[B-series battle droid/Legends|droid]] [[EEK-176]], and together they attacked New Centrif from its less defended southern entrance and razed the city and its transmitter.

==Description==
{{Quote|New Centrif seems to be well defended.|OOM-9|audio=NewCentrifStrong.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
New Centrif was a [[City/Legends|city]] on the<ref name="GB" /> [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], loyal to the [[Royal House of Naboo/Legends|Royal House of Naboo]] and inhabited by the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] people. It was located west of the city [[Vis/Legends|Vis]] and north of the city [[Parrlay]] and was bordered by [[Forest/Legends|forests]] on the eastern and western sides. A paved path led into the northern entrance of the city, which was connected to Vis by a dirt track, and the southern entrance connected a paved road down to Parrlay and another path stretching north around the forest to the southern entrance of Vis. The ground in New Centrif was covered in pavement and [[Grass/Legends|grass]], and the buildings and walls were consistently decorated with [[Color/Legends|orange]] patterns.<ref name="GB" />

[[File:South_New_Centrif.png|thumb|left|300px|South New Centrif]]
Two flags were placed ahead of the northern entrance,<ref name="GB" /> which resembled a cul-de-sac aside from the gate.<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> Two lines of light walls stretched northward from the gate. Inside the entrance, two defensive [[Turret/Legends|turrets]] were built, giving New Centrif a well-defended appearance. Toward the west of the entrance were a small [[Water well|well]] and a gazebo. [[Tree/Legends|Trees]] and [[Flower/Legends|flowers]] grew throughout that decorative area, and a small light wall and several trees served as a border between it and the rest of the city. Two residential buildings were located south of the gate along with a [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitter]] that supported off-world communications. The transmitter was placed on a raised piece of land, was surrounded by a light wall, and was also powered by a [[Power core/Legends|power core]] close to the western forest.<ref name="GB" />

The center of the city was occupied by a monument, and northeast of it was a [[Barracks/Legends|troop center]] and two more residential buildings. On the center-east, a power core was built, which supported the [[Trooper/Legends|troop]] center and two residential buildings toward the southwest and one in the northeast corner. A small light wall was situated southeast between the buildings and the forest. At the southern area of New Centrif was a [[food processing center]], where the [[Foodstuff/Legends|goods]] from three [[Farm/Legends|farms]] that were harvested by [[PKN-49 worker droid]]s were stored. In the southeastern side was a [[nova processing center]], where more [[Droid/Legends|droids]] collected [[Nova crystal/Legends|nova crystals]] from a nearby stockpile. Southwest of the farms, an [[ore processing center]] gathered [[Ore/Legends|ore]] from another stockpile.<ref name="GB" />

==History==
{{Quote|We have secured New Centrif.|EEK-176|audio=NewCentrifSecure.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Naboo was [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invaded]] by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], which initially targeted the transmitters in New Centrif, Vis, and Parrlay to prevent word of their invasion from reaching the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] sent the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droid]] [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] with a small task force of the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]], which included eight [[B-series battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], four heavy [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|beetle droid]] units, and two [[Single Trooper Aerial Platform/Legends|Single Trooper Aerial Platform]] units to destroy the transmitters. OOM-9's forces landed a [[C-9979 landing craft/Legends|C-9979 landing craft]] north of New Centrif and marched toward the city. At the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the invasion, New Centrif was defended by a total of seventeen troopers,<ref name="GB" /> five [[Tuskcat Trooper]]s,<ref name="CC">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''</ref> and three [[V-19 landspeeder]]s.<ref name="GB" />

[[File:New_Centrif_attack.png|thumb|right|300px|New Centrif under attack by the Trade Federation]]
As the [[Battle droid/Legends|droids]] drew nearer, OOM-9 observed that New Centrif was well defended and that reinforcements would be required before taking the city. [[Gender/Legends|He]] turned the [[Army/Legends|army]] on the path east to Vis, where they were joined with reinforcements led by the droid [[EEK-176]], and together they [[Subjugation of Parrlay|destroyed]] the Vis transmitter.<ref name="GB" /> The combined forces took the route that connected Vis to the south of New Centrif, which was less defended than the main northern entrance due to a lack of gates and walls. At the city, the droids gunned down the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] and [[Labor droid/Legends|labor droids]] near the nova crystal deposits, at which point the vast majority of New Centrif's population descended upon the invaders. OOM-9 fought them off with the battle droids and his personal [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Heavy Armored Assault Tank]], keeping his beetle droids in reserve. With New Centrif's inhabitants [[Death/Legends|killed]], OOM-9 sent the beetle droids in to raze the city, including the transmitter.<ref name="Prima" /> EEK-176 immediately reported the result to Gunray.<ref name="GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:New_Centrif_Prima_guide.png|thumb|right|275px|An alternate depiction of New Centrif from ''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide'', featuring a spaceport instead of a transmitter]]
New Centrif was first mentioned in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'' by [[Steven L. Kent]],<ref name="Prima" /> released on [[November 7]], [[2001]],<ref name="PrimaDate">{{Amazon|url=Star-Wars-Galactic-Battlegrounds-Official/dp/0761537503|text=Star Wars Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide}}</ref> before appearing in the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'',<ref name="GB" /> released on [[November 11]] the same year.<ref name="GBDate">{{GameSpot|url=games/star-wars-galactic-battlegrounds|text=Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds}}</ref> The city appears in the first level of the Trade Federation campaign, entitled "Beachhead," where the player controls the Trade Federation forces.<ref name="GB" /> The strategy guide states that if the player attempts to attack New Centrif straight away without gaining reinforcements, they would have a difficult time winning the level since the city entrance can trap the player's units in a small area.<ref name="Prima" />

The guide also describes the objective of the level as destroying [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]] in the Naboo cities instead of transmitters, with the book including a picture of a spaceport in the location<ref name="Prima" /> that the transmitter occupied in the game. The turrets at the entrance are not present if the player attempts the level in easy mode but are present in moderate and hard difficulties. Three of the royal troopers and two V-19 landspeeders are also only present in the hard difficulty. In the original version of ''Galactic Battlegrounds'', the mounted Naboo troopers rode [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]],<ref name="GB" /> which were replaced with [[tusk cat]]s in the [[2002]] expansion, [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|''Clone Campaigns'']].<ref name="CC" />
